fix(docker): exclude NVIDIA CUDA packages from uv sync

- Skip torch and 16 nvidia-* packages in uv sync since pip installs CPU-only torch
- Reduces Docker image size by ~4GB by avoiding CUDA libraries
- Fixes deployment failure due to disk space exhaustion on VPS

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(docker): use CPU-only PyTorch to reduce image size by ~4GB

Install PyTorch from CPU-only index before other dependencies.
The server doesn't have a GPU, so CUDA libraries are unnecessary.
This reduces the API image from ~5GB to ~1GB.

public directory exists after checkout in CI, fixing the Docker
COPY command that failed with "not found" error.

commit.message:
feat: add conversation persistence with URL params and click-to-open

Backend:
- Fix list_conversations SQL query (remove non-existent thread_ts column)
- Add get_conversation_message_count function
- Expose X-Conversation-Id header via CORS

Frontend:
- Add URL-based conversation persistence (?conversation=id)
- Load conversation history on page refresh
- Click conversation row to open in playground
- Upgrade Next.js to 16.1.1

feat: add simba-chat widget package and refactor playground chat components

- Replace ai-elements with streamlined chat components (chat-container, chat-message, etc.)
- Add simba-chat NPM package for embeddable chat widget
- Add Claude Code configuration (CLAUDE.md, setup command)
- Update Makefile and documentation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: integrate collection selection and error handling in chat functionality

- Added collection selection to the PlaygroundPage, allowing users to choose a collection for chat interactions.
- Implemented auto-selection of the first available collection upon loading.
- Enhanced the useChat hook to manage collection state and handle errors during message processing.
- Updated chat service to support collection-specific queries, improving the relevance of responses.
- Improved UI components to reflect the selected collection and provide user feedback on the chat experience.

feat: enhance chat functionality with new components and improved UI

- Integrated new AI elements for conversation management, including sources and reasoning displays.
- Refactored PlaygroundPage to utilize the useChat hook for better state management and message handling.
- Updated UI components for a more streamlined chat experience, including a new input method and loading indicators.
- Added support for tool invocation and message streaming, enhancing real-time interaction in the chat interface.
- Introduced new CardAction component for better layout control in UI.

feat: implement streaming chat responses with SSE support

- Refactored chat endpoint to utilize a new chat_stream service for streaming responses.
- Enhanced chat_stream function to yield SSE-formatted events, including model thinking, tool invocation, and AI response chunks.
- Updated dependencies to support the new streaming functionality, improving real-time interaction in conversations.

feat: add document chunks viewing functionality and enhance document status polling

- Introduced a button to view document chunks in the DocumentsTable component, enhancing user interaction.
- Implemented a ChunksViewer modal to display chunks of a selected document.
- Updated the useDocuments hook to include an auto-refresh feature for polling document statuses every 3 seconds when documents are processing.
- Added new API endpoints for retrieving document statuses and chunks, improving backend support for document management.

feat: enhance dashboard analytics and conversations management

- Implemented loading states and dynamic data fetching for analytics metrics, improving user experience with real-time updates.
- Added conversation search functionality and delete confirmation for better conversation management.
- Introduced collection selection for document management, allowing users to organize documents effectively.
- Updated API routes and types to support new features, including analytics and conversation handling.

feat: enhance Docker setup with PostgreSQL service and update CLI commands

- Added PostgreSQL service to docker-compose.yml with health checks and environment variables for user, password, and database name.
- Updated CLI to allow the 'serve' alias for the server command, improving usability.
- Modified database URL in configuration to reflect the new PostgreSQL setup.

refactor: update Docker configuration and streamline service dependencies

- Modified docker-compose.yml to adjust service configurations, including changing the server port from 5005 to 8000 and updating environment variables for Qdrant and MinIO.
- Added build context for the server and celery_worker services to utilize a Dockerfile.
- Enhanced health checks for Redis, Qdrant, and MinIO services to ensure they are healthy before starting dependent services.
- Simplified the Dockerfile by removing unnecessary build stages and dependencies, focusing on essential packages for document parsing and application runtime.
- Updated the default command to run the API server on port 8000.

feat: enhance document management and integration with Qdrant and MinIO

- Added new dependencies for Qdrant and MinIO to support vector storage and object storage functionalities.
- Updated Docker Compose configuration to include services for Qdrant and MinIO with health checks and storage volumes.
- Refactored document management routes to support uploading, listing, and deleting documents with integration to MinIO for file storage and Qdrant for vector management.
- Implemented document upload and processing logic, including validation and error handling.
- Enhanced API routes for document retrieval and reprocessing, ensuring better user feedback and functionality.

chore: remove obsolete files and configurations

- Deleted various unused files including configuration, documentation, and example scripts.
- Removed legacy database migration scripts and obsolete API routes to streamline the codebase.
- Cleaned up frontend assets and components that are no longer in use.
- Updated project structure for better organization and maintainability.
feat: enhancement in UI summarization bulk actions ingestion and celery tasks (#78)

commit.message: chore: retrigger CI build
chore: retrigger CI build
ci: added build phase

Release

* feat: auth with supabase

* feat: implement auth system with roles, permissions and organizations

- Added authentication using Supabase with support for both cloud and local mode
- Implemented comprehensive role-based access control system with permissions
- Created organization management with member roles and invitations
- Refactored database access to use PostgresDB connection pooling for better performance

* feat: UI UX improvement

* feat: add settings management with general and members settings pages

- Introduced a new SettingsPage component to manage organization settings.
- Added GeneralSettings and MembersSettings components for detailed settings management.
- Updated routing to include new settings structure and redirect legacy routes.
- Removed outdated sidebar items related to roles and organizations for cleaner navigation.

* feat: migrate to PostgreSQL with pgvector support

- Updated configuration to switch from SQLite to PostgreSQL as the database provider.
- Changed vector store implementation from FAISS to pgvector for enhanced vector similarity search capabilities.
- Introduced new database and vector store classes to support PostgreSQL integration.
- Added migration scripts to create necessary database tables and extensions for pgvector.
- Removed outdated SQLite service and related files to streamline the codebase.
- Added sample questions for chatbot evaluation to improve testing and validation.

* feat: add text handling methods to PGVectorStore

- Introduced  method in  for adding texts to the vector store.
- Implemented  method in  to handle adding multiple texts with optional metadata and IDs.
- Updated  method name for clarity in functionality.
- Enhanced error handling and logging for text addition operations.

* fix: update retrieval method and parameter naming for consistency

- Changed the retrieval method in the configuration from hybrid to default for clarity.
- Updated the retrieval calls in the codebase to use default instead of hybrid.
- Renamed parameter k to top_k in multiple retriever classes to standardize naming conventions.
- Ensured consistent usage of top_k across different retrieval implementations for improved readability and maintainability.

* feat: enhance document ingestion and access control

- Updated ingestion API to include user authentication, ensuring that document operations (ingest, update, retrieve, delete) are user-specific.
- Modified database schema to include a user_id field in the documents table for tracking ownership.
- Implemented row-level security policies in PostgreSQL to restrict access to documents based on the authenticated user.
- Enhanced error handling to provide clearer feedback when access is denied or documents are not found.
- Updated .gitignore to include cursor files for cleaner project management.

* feat: implement user-specific document retrieval and access control

- Added user_id parameter to various retriever classes to support multi-tenant filtering.
- Updated database schema to include user_id in the chunks_embeddings table for ownership tracking.
- Enhanced retrieval methods to ensure documents are filtered by user_id, improving security and access control.
- Implemented row-level security policies in PostgreSQL to restrict access based on user_id.
- Updated vector store service methods to include user_id for secure document searches.

* chore: remove obsolete migration and RBAC setup scripts

- Deleted the apply_migrations.py script, which was responsible for applying database migrations to the Supabase database.
- Removed the sample_questions.csv file, which contained example questions for chatbot evaluation.
- Eliminated the setup_rbac.py script that set up the role-based access control schema in the Supabase database.
- These deletions streamline the codebase by removing unused or redundant files.

* feat: implement API key management and authentication

- Added API key management functionality, allowing users to create, retrieve, deactivate, and validate API keys.
- Introduced a new APIKeyService for handling API key operations and integrated it with the existing authentication system.
- Updated middleware to support authentication via API keys in addition to bearer tokens.
- Created a new database migration to establish the api_keys table with appropriate security policies.
- Enhanced the application structure by including API key routes and integrating them into the main application.
- Added tests for the new ChatManager and SimbaClient functionalities to ensure proper behavior.

* feat: enhance document splitting and retrieval in embedding service

- Integrated a new Splitter class into the EmbeddingService to handle document chunking with configurable strategies.
- Updated the Splitter class to support multiple chunking strategies, including semantic chunking and markdown header splitting.
- Enhanced the similarity_search method in PGVectorStore to include an optional first-pass retrieval using BM25 for improved document retrieval efficiency.
- Added support for document ID metadata in test cases to ensure accurate tracking and retrieval.
- Improved error handling in the Splitter class to validate input document types.

* feat: update embedding service and splitter for improved document handling

- Increased chunk size and overlap in the Splitter class to enhance document processing capabilities.
- Added unique ID assignment for split documents to ensure proper tracking and management.
- Adjusted user_id handling in PGVectorStore to ensure consistent string formatting.
- Modified the default top_k parameter in similarity_search method for optimized retrieval performance.

* chore: update configuration and enhance error handling in settings

- fixed migration issue
- added self corrective rag
- Changed embedding model from "text-embedding-3-small" to "text-embedding-3-large" for improved performance.
- Updated device setting in configuration for better compatibility with container environments.
- Enhanced error handling in GeneralSettings component to provide clearer feedback when API calls are disabled in development mode.
- Refactored organization fetching logic to improve clarity and maintainability.
- Added helper function to check owner permissions and streamline member management in organization routes.

* fix: update embedding configuration and fix document deletion logic

- Changed embedding model from "text-embedding-3-large" to "text-embedding-ada-002" for enhanced performance.
- Updated device setting in configuration for better compatibility with container environments.
- Simplified document deletion logic in EmbeddingService to directly delete by document ID instead of a list of IDs.
- Increased vector size in PGVector class from 1024 to 1536 for improved embedding capacity.

* feat: enhance state management and message handling

- Added 'generation' field to the State class for client-facing state representation.
- Updated generate_node to return 'generation' alongside documents and messages.
- Modified grade_node, hallucination_node, and transform_query to extract the question from the last message content instead of the state question field.

* feat: improved RAG pipeline with hybrid search advacned

-Use raw SQL in SQLAlchemy to query with <=>
-removed create index function and ivfflat
-we use HSNW with pgvector
-reranker for french
- add correctness chain for response grading
- Introduced a new correctness_chain to evaluate if responses address user questions effectively.
- Implemented a GradeCorrectness data model for binary scoring of relevance.
- Integrated correctness_chain into hallucination_node for improved grading functionality.

* fix: improve document grading output in grade_node

- Updated grade_node to include document index in grading output for better traceability.
- Enhanced readability of relevance messages by specifying the document number in the logs.

* feat: add dropdown menu for document management actions

- Replaced the Plus icon with a MoreVertical icon for better UI representation.
- Introduced a DropdownMenu component to allow users to configure the system directly from the header.
- Enhanced the DocumentManagementHeader for improved user interaction and accessibility.

* feat: add Knowledge Configuration page and update sidebar

- Introduced a new KnowledgeConfigPage component for configuring language models, document processing, embedding, and retrieval settings.
- Added a route for the Knowledge Configuration page in App.tsx.
- Updated the sidebar to include a link to the Knowledge Configuration page with an appropriate icon.

* feat: add react-hot-toast and refactor Knowledge Configuration routing

- Added  for improved user notifications.
- Refactored routing for the Knowledge Configuration page, changing the path from  to .
- Updated sidebar to reflect the new path for the Knowledge Configuration page.
- Removed the old KnowledgeConfigPage component and replaced it with a new implementation in the pages directory.

* feat: refactor organization routes to use Supabase client and enhance error handling

- Replaced OrganizationService with Supabase client for organization data retrieval and management.
- Updated user type from UserResponse to dict for better flexibility.
- Improved error handling for organization retrieval, creation, and member management to provide clearer feedback.
- Implemented Row Level Security (RLS) policies for organization and member actions to enforce permissions.
- Removed legacy SQL migration files related to roles and permissions as they are no longer needed.

* feat: update configuration for LLM and embedding providers, enhance KnowledgeConfigPage

- Changed LLM provider from OpenAI to Ollama and updated model name to llama3.1:8b.
- Updated embedding provider from OpenAI to Hugging Face with a new model name.
- Added chunking configuration to KnowledgeConfigPage for better document processing.
- Improved error handling in organization routes to provide clearer feedback on response issues.
- Introduced new SQL migration for service role grants to enhance database permissions.

* fix: implement Row Level Security (RLS) policies for organizations and organization members

- Added new SQL migration files to establish RLS policies for organizations and organization members, ensuring proper access control based on user roles.
- Created helper functions to check membership and roles, preventing recursion issues in policy evaluations.
- Updated permissions for authenticated and anonymous users in the schema to enhance security and access management.
- Restructured policies to allow for clearer membership checks and role-based access, improving overall data integrity and security.

* feat: add Redis caching for document management and browser-side caching

* feat: implement API key management features and enhance tenant support

- Added new API key management routes and services, allowing users to create, delete, and deactivate API keys.
- Introduced tenant ID support for API keys, enabling multi-tenant architecture.
- Updated API key validation and retrieval methods to include tenant context.
- Enhanced error handling and logging for API key operations.
- Created a new ApiKeysPage component for managing API keys in the frontend.
- Updated axios configuration to handle authentication and token refresh seamlessly.
- Added SQL migration to include tenant_id in the api_keys table for better data organization.

* Revert "feat: add Redis caching for document management and browser-side caching"

This reverts commit 4968d92310561608edba6dd73dd59f443c4bb47b.

* fix: removed redis cache management from ingestion

* Remove redis.py file

* feat: refactor front api services to use axios instead of request

* feat: update API key management and settings routes

- Refactored API key service to use a new HTTP client.
- Removed unused folder API service and updated related imports.
- Added API Keys route to the settings page and adjusted routing for better navigation.
- Enhanced error handling in API key creation process to ensure proper logging and feedback.

* feat: update organization management and settings

- Changed LLM provider from Ollama to OpenAI and updated model names in config.yaml.
- Added OrganizationsSettings component for managing organizations in the settings page.
- Refactored Navbar to remove organization selection logic and link to manage organizations.
- Integrated organization API services for fetching, creating, and managing organizations.
- Updated OrganizationPage to utilize new organization API services for data handling.
- Enhanced role management and member invitations within organizations.
- Implemented SQL migrations for organization-related features and access control.

* chore: add cursorignore

* chore: update dependencies and enhance chatbot functionality

- Updated Poetry lock file to reflect changes in package versions, including aiohappyeyeballs, aiohttp, youtube-transcript-api, and others.
- Added new dependencies: pgvector and email-validator.
- Introduced new nodes for the chatbot workflow: rerank and compress, enhancing document processing capabilities.
- Updated state management to track reranked and compressed documents, improving context handling for LLM generation.
- Implemented logic to limit query transformation attempts to prevent infinite loops.

* chore: streamline document grading and generation logic

- Removed the transform_query node from the workflow to simplify decision-making.
- Updated the grade function to work with compressed documents instead of original documents.
- Enhanced the decide_to_generate function to clarify decision-making based on document relevance.
- Adjusted workflow edges to reflect the new logic for generating responses.

verification.payload:
tree 88bf46358dc1b56f8b8b67df5a6770a150187d3c
parent 47497231057630403ff9917239787df5749fe6c5
author Hamza Zerouali <43603227+GitHamza0206@users.noreply.github.com> 1747005227 +0200
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1747005227 +0200

Release

* feat: auth with supabase

* feat: implement auth system with roles, permissions and organizations

- Added authentication using Supabase with support for both cloud and local mode
- Implemented comprehensive role-based access control system with permissions
- Created organization management with member roles and invitations
- Refactored database access to use PostgresDB connection pooling for better performance

* feat: UI UX improvement

* feat: add settings management with general and members settings pages

- Introduced a new SettingsPage component to manage organization settings.
- Added GeneralSettings and MembersSettings components for detailed settings management.
- Updated routing to include new settings structure and redirect legacy routes.
- Removed outdated sidebar items related to roles and organizations for cleaner navigation.

* feat: migrate to PostgreSQL with pgvector support

- Updated configuration to switch from SQLite to PostgreSQL as the database provider.
- Changed vector store implementation from FAISS to pgvector for enhanced vector similarity search capabilities.
- Introduced new database and vector store classes to support PostgreSQL integration.
- Added migration scripts to create necessary database tables and extensions for pgvector.
- Removed outdated SQLite service and related files to streamline the codebase.
- Added sample questions for chatbot evaluation to improve testing and validation.

* feat: add text handling methods to PGVectorStore

- Introduced  method in  for adding texts to the vector store.
- Implemented  method in  to handle adding multiple texts with optional metadata and IDs.
- Updated  method name for clarity in functionality.
- Enhanced error handling and logging for text addition operations.

* fix: update retrieval method and parameter naming for consistency

- Changed the retrieval method in the configuration from hybrid to default for clarity.
- Updated the retrieval calls in the codebase to use default instead of hybrid.
- Renamed parameter k to top_k in multiple retriever classes to standardize naming conventions.
- Ensured consistent usage of top_k across different retrieval implementations for improved readability and maintainability.

* feat: enhance document ingestion and access control

- Updated ingestion API to include user authentication, ensuring that document operations (ingest, update, retrieve, delete) are user-specific.
- Modified database schema to include a user_id field in the documents table for tracking ownership.
- Implemented row-level security policies in PostgreSQL to restrict access to documents based on the authenticated user.
- Enhanced error handling to provide clearer feedback when access is denied or documents are not found.
- Updated .gitignore to include cursor files for cleaner project management.

* feat: implement user-specific document retrieval and access control

- Added user_id parameter to various retriever classes to support multi-tenant filtering.
- Updated database schema to include user_id in the chunks_embeddings table for ownership tracking.
- Enhanced retrieval methods to ensure documents are filtered by user_id, improving security and access control.
- Implemented row-level security policies in PostgreSQL to restrict access based on user_id.
- Updated vector store service methods to include user_id for secure document searches.

* chore: remove obsolete migration and RBAC setup scripts

- Deleted the apply_migrations.py script, which was responsible for applying database migrations to the Supabase database.
- Removed the sample_questions.csv file, which contained example questions for chatbot evaluation.
- Eliminated the setup_rbac.py script that set up the role-based access control schema in the Supabase database.
- These deletions streamline the codebase by removing unused or redundant files.

* feat: implement API key management and authentication

- Added API key management functionality, allowing users to create, retrieve, deactivate, and validate API keys.
- Introduced a new APIKeyService for handling API key operations and integrated it with the existing authentication system.
- Updated middleware to support authentication via API keys in addition to bearer tokens.
- Created a new database migration to establish the api_keys table with appropriate security policies.
- Enhanced the application structure by including API key routes and integrating them into the main application.
- Added tests for the new ChatManager and SimbaClient functionalities to ensure proper behavior.

* feat: enhance document splitting and retrieval in embedding service

- Integrated a new Splitter class into the EmbeddingService to handle document chunking with configurable strategies.
- Updated the Splitter class to support multiple chunking strategies, including semantic chunking and markdown header splitting.
- Enhanced the similarity_search method in PGVectorStore to include an optional first-pass retrieval using BM25 for improved document retrieval efficiency.
- Added support for document ID metadata in test cases to ensure accurate tracking and retrieval.
- Improved error handling in the Splitter class to validate input document types.

* feat: update embedding service and splitter for improved document handling

- Increased chunk size and overlap in the Splitter class to enhance document processing capabilities.
- Added unique ID assignment for split documents to ensure proper tracking and management.
- Adjusted user_id handling in PGVectorStore to ensure consistent string formatting.
- Modified the default top_k parameter in similarity_search method for optimized retrieval performance.

* chore: update configuration and enhance error handling in settings

- fixed migration issue
- added self corrective rag
- Changed embedding model from "text-embedding-3-small" to "text-embedding-3-large" for improved performance.
- Updated device setting in configuration for better compatibility with container environments.
- Enhanced error handling in GeneralSettings component to provide clearer feedback when API calls are disabled in development mode.
- Refactored organization fetching logic to improve clarity and maintainability.
- Added helper function to check owner permissions and streamline member management in organization routes.

* fix: update embedding configuration and fix document deletion logic

- Changed embedding model from "text-embedding-3-large" to "text-embedding-ada-002" for enhanced performance.
- Updated device setting in configuration for better compatibility with container environments.
- Simplified document deletion logic in EmbeddingService to directly delete by document ID instead of a list of IDs.
- Increased vector size in PGVector class from 1024 to 1536 for improved embedding capacity.

* feat: enhance state management and message handling

- Added 'generation' field to the State class for client-facing state representation.
- Updated generate_node to return 'generation' alongside documents and messages.
- Modified grade_node, hallucination_node, and transform_query to extract the question from the last message content instead of the state question field.

* feat: improved RAG pipeline with hybrid search advacned

-Use raw SQL in SQLAlchemy to query with <=>
-removed create index function and ivfflat
-we use HSNW with pgvector
-reranker for french
- add correctness chain for response grading
- Introduced a new correctness_chain to evaluate if responses address user questions effectively.
- Implemented a GradeCorrectness data model for binary scoring of relevance.
- Integrated correctness_chain into hallucination_node for improved grading functionality.

* fix: improve document grading output in grade_node

- Updated grade_node to include document index in grading output for better traceability.
- Enhanced readability of relevance messages by specifying the document number in the logs.

* feat: add dropdown menu for document management actions

- Replaced the Plus icon with a MoreVertical icon for better UI representation.
- Introduced a DropdownMenu component to allow users to configure the system directly from the header.
- Enhanced the DocumentManagementHeader for improved user interaction and accessibility.

* feat: add Knowledge Configuration page and update sidebar

- Introduced a new KnowledgeConfigPage component for configuring language models, document processing, embedding, and retrieval settings.
- Added a route for the Knowledge Configuration page in App.tsx.
- Updated the sidebar to include a link to the Knowledge Configuration page with an appropriate icon.

* feat: add react-hot-toast and refactor Knowledge Configuration routing

- Added  for improved user notifications.
- Refactored routing for the Knowledge Configuration page, changing the path from  to .
- Updated sidebar to reflect the new path for the Knowledge Configuration page.
- Removed the old KnowledgeConfigPage component and replaced it with a new implementation in the pages directory.

* feat: refactor organization routes to use Supabase client and enhance error handling

- Replaced OrganizationService with Supabase client for organization data retrieval and management.
- Updated user type from UserResponse to dict for better flexibility.
- Improved error handling for organization retrieval, creation, and member management to provide clearer feedback.
- Implemented Row Level Security (RLS) policies for organization and member actions to enforce permissions.
- Removed legacy SQL migration files related to roles and permissions as they are no longer needed.

* feat: update configuration for LLM and embedding providers, enhance KnowledgeConfigPage

- Changed LLM provider from OpenAI to Ollama and updated model name to llama3.1:8b.
- Updated embedding provider from OpenAI to Hugging Face with a new model name.
- Added chunking configuration to KnowledgeConfigPage for better document processing.
- Improved error handling in organization routes to provide clearer feedback on response issues.
- Introduced new SQL migration for service role grants to enhance database permissions.

* fix: implement Row Level Security (RLS) policies for organizations and organization members

- Added new SQL migration files to establish RLS policies for organizations and organization members, ensuring proper access control based on user roles.
- Created helper functions to check membership and roles, preventing recursion issues in policy evaluations.
- Updated permissions for authenticated and anonymous users in the schema to enhance security and access management.
- Restructured policies to allow for clearer membership checks and role-based access, improving overall data integrity and security.

* feat: add Redis caching for document management and browser-side caching

* feat: implement API key management features and enhance tenant support

- Added new API key management routes and services, allowing users to create, delete, and deactivate API keys.
- Introduced tenant ID support for API keys, enabling multi-tenant architecture.
- Updated API key validation and retrieval methods to include tenant context.
- Enhanced error handling and logging for API key operations.
- Created a new ApiKeysPage component for managing API keys in the frontend.
- Updated axios configuration to handle authentication and token refresh seamlessly.
- Added SQL migration to include tenant_id in the api_keys table for better data organization.

* Revert "feat: add Redis caching for document management and browser-side caching"

This reverts commit 4968d92310561608edba6dd73dd59f443c4bb47b.

* fix: removed redis cache management from ingestion

* Remove redis.py file

* feat: refactor front api services to use axios instead of request

* feat: update API key management and settings routes

- Refactored API key service to use a new HTTP client.
- Removed unused folder API service and updated related imports.
- Added API Keys route to the settings page and adjusted routing for better navigation.
- Enhanced error handling in API key creation process to ensure proper logging and feedback.

* fix: UI collection tab creation

* feat: update organization management and settings

- Changed LLM provider from Ollama to OpenAI and updated model names in config.yaml.
- Added OrganizationsSettings component for managing organizations in the settings page.
- Refactored Navbar to remove organization selection logic and link to manage organizations.
- Integrated organization API services for fetching, creating, and managing organizations.
- Updated OrganizationPage to utilize new organization API services for data handling.
- Enhanced role management and member invitations within organizations.
- Implemented SQL migrations for organization-related features and access control.

* chore: add cursorignore

* chore: update dependencies and enhance chatbot functionality

- Updated Poetry lock file to reflect changes in package versions, including aiohappyeyeballs, aiohttp, youtube-transcript-api, and others.
- Added new dependencies: pgvector and email-validator.
- Introduced new nodes for the chatbot workflow: rerank and compress, enhancing document processing capabilities.
- Updated state management to track reranked and compressed documents, improving context handling for LLM generation.
- Implemented logic to limit query transformation attempts to prevent infinite loops.

* chore: streamline document grading and generation logic

- Removed the transform_query node from the workflow to simplify decision-making.
- Updated the grade function to work with compressed documents instead of original documents.
- Enhanced the decide_to_generate function to clarify decision-making based on document relevance.
- Adjusted workflow edges to reflect the new logic for generating responses.

* chore: removed grader

* style: improved chat layout

* style: improved chat frame

readme_html:
See how easy it is to integrate Simba into your website

What is Simba?

Simba is an open-source customer service assistant built for teams who need full control over their AI. Unlike black-box solutions, Simba is designed from the ground up around evaluation and customization, so you can measure performance, iterate fast, and tailor the assistant to your exact needs.

Why Simba?

Problem Simba's Solution
Can't measure AI quality Built-in evaluation framework with retrieval and generation metrics
Generic responses Fully customizable RAG pipeline with your own data
Hard to integrate Drop-in npm package for instant website integration
Vendor lock-in Open-source, self-hosted, swap any component

Key Features

  • Evaluation-First Design - Track retrieval accuracy, generation quality, and latency out of the box. Know exactly how your assistant performs.
  • Fully Customizable - Swap embedding models, LLMs, vector stores, chunking strategies, and rerankers. Your pipeline, your rules.
  • npm Package for Easy Integration - Add a customer service chat widget to your website with a single npm install.
  • Modern Dashboard - Manage documents, monitor conversations, and analyze performance from a clean UI.
  • Production-Ready - Streaming responses, async processing, and scalable architecture.

Quick Start

Docker (Recommended)

The fastest way to get Simba running:

git clone https://github.com/GitHamza0206/simba.git
cd simba

Create a .env file:

OPENAI_API_KEY=your_openai_api_key

Run with Docker:

# CPU
DEVICE=cpu make build && make up

# NVIDIA GPU
DEVICE=cuda make build && make up

Visit http://localhost:3000 to access the dashboard.

Manual Installation

If you prefer installing without Docker:

pip install simba-core

simba server
simba front

Development Setup with Claude Code

If you're using Claude Code, you can set up the project with a single command:

/setup --all

This will automatically install all dependencies (Python, frontend, npm package) and start the infrastructure services. Other options:

/setup --backend    # Python dependencies only
/setup --frontend   # Next.js + simba-chat only
/setup --services   # Start Docker infrastructure only

Website Integration

Add Simba to your website with the npm package:

npm install simba-chat-widget

import { SimbaChat } from 'simba-chat-widget';

function App() {
  return (
    <SimbaChat
      apiUrl="https://your-simba-instance.com"
      theme="light"
    />
  );
}

That's it. Your customers now have an AI assistant powered by your knowledge base.

Evaluation & Metrics

Simba tracks what matters:

  • Retrieval Metrics - Precision, recall, relevance scores
  • Generation Metrics - Faithfulness, answer relevancy, latency
  • Conversation Analytics - User satisfaction, resolution rates

Use these metrics to continuously improve your assistant's performance.

Architecture

┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
│   Your Website  │────▶│   Simba API     │────▶│  Vector Store   │
│  (npm package)  │     │   (FastAPI)     │     │  (Qdrant/FAISS) │
└─────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘
                               │                        ▲
                               │                        │
                               ▼                        │
                        ┌─────────────────┐     ┌───────┴─────────┐
                        │      LLM        │     │     Celery      │
                        │ (OpenAI/Local)  │     │   (Ingestion)   │
                        └─────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘
                                                        │
                                                        ▼
                                                ┌─────────────────┐
                                                │      Redis      │
                                                │  (Task Queue)   │
                                                └─────────────────┘
